name: Death Certificate
description: An official government document declaring the cause, location, and time of death, required to settle legal obligations and inheritance.
application_fee: Free (within 21 days), Late fees apply thereafter
eligibility: Immediate relatives or head of the family.
application_steps: If death occurred in a hospital, collect the medical certificate of cause of death; the hospital will notify the registrar.
If death occurred at home, report it to the local municipal/panchayat registrar within 21 days.
Submit the death registration form along with the deceased's ID and cremation/burial receipt.
Collect the death certificate from the registrar's office after processing.

required_documents.name: Proof of Death
required_documents.purpose: Medical or administrative confirmation of death.

required_documents.name: Deceased's Identity Proof
required_documents.purpose: To officially identify the deceased person.
required_documents.where_to_get: Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, PAN Card

common_mistakes: Delaying registration beyond 21 days, which requires a Magistrate's order.
Discrepancies in the deceased's name between the hospital record and their Aadhaar.
Failing to obtain the cremation ground receipt.
